machinery_genes.quote: ATP binding triggers oligomerization of Xanthomonas campestris XpsE (a GspE homolog) as well as its association with the N-terminal domain of XpsL (a GspL homolog)
machinery_genes.note: Shiue et al 2006 (EMBO J). XpsE = the GspE-family T2S ATPase of Xcc; further characterized in Shiue et al 2007 Mol Microbiol (PMID 17630971) and Chen & Hu 2013 PLoS One (PMID 23536861).

machinery_genes.quote: the Xanthomonas campestris extracellular enzyme secretion factor XpsF
machinery_genes.note: Kaufman et al 1993 (Gene); XpsF named as the X. campestris extracellular-secretion (T2S) factor of the GspF/PulF family. Also listed in the xpsEFGHIJKLMND operon (Lee et al 2001, PMID 11133946).

machinery_genes.quote: we performed biochemical analyses of the XpsG protein of Xanthomonas campestris pv. campestris and observed that it is a pillar-like structure spanning the cytoplasmic and outer membranes
machinery_genes.note: Hu et al 2002 (Biochem J); XpsG = major pseudopilin (GspG family: 'GspG, -H, -I, -J and -K proteins are members of the pseudopilin family').

machinery_genes.quote: a linearly ordered interactive relationship is revealed among the four pseudopilins, XpsG-XpsI-XpsH-XpsJ
machinery_genes.note: Kuo et al 2005 (J Biomed Sci); XpsH named as a minor pseudopilin of the Xcc T2S pathway (GspH family).

machinery_genes.quote: a linearly ordered interactive relationship is revealed among the four pseudopilins, XpsG-XpsI-XpsH-XpsJ
machinery_genes.note: Kuo et al 2005; XpsI named as a minor pseudopilin of the Xcc T2S pathway (GspI family).

machinery_genes.quote: we investigate the mutual interaction among the pseudopilins and characterize the specialized minor pseudopilin, XpsJ
machinery_genes.note: Kuo et al 2005; XpsJ named as a minor pseudopilin (GspJ family), proposed to cap the pseudopilus tip.

machinery_genes.quote: The xps gene cluster (xpsEFGHIJKLMND) was deleted
machinery_genes.note: Lee et al 2001 (J Bacteriol). xpsK is named as one of the eleven ORFs of the Xcc xps T2S gene cluster; assigned GspK minor-pseudopilin role from the GspG/H/I/J/K pseudopilin family (Hu et al 2002, PMID 11931643). No per-gene functional-characterization quote found, so lowest-confidence call in this set.

machinery_genes.quote: The cytoplasmic membrane proteins XpsL, XpsM and XpsN are components required for type II secretion in Xanthomonas campestris pv. campestris
machinery_genes.note: Tsai et al 2002 (Biochem J); XpsL = GspL-family IM platform; its N-terminal cytoplasmic domain recruits the ATPase XpsE (Shiue et al 2006, PMID 16525507).

machinery_genes.quote: The cytoplasmic membrane proteins XpsL, XpsM and XpsN are components required for type II secretion in Xanthomonas campestris pv. campestris
machinery_genes.note: Tsai et al 2002 (Biochem J); XpsM = GspM-family IM platform, forms a ternary XpsL-XpsM-XpsN complex.

machinery_genes.role: inner-membrane platform component (GspC; links IM platform to secretin)
machinery_genes.quote: XpsL, XpsM, and XpsN are components of such machinery in the plant pathogen ... pv. campestris
machinery_genes.note: Lee et al 2004 (J Bacteriol), titled 'Functional dissection of the XpsN (GspC) protein'; XpsN = the GspC homolog of the Xcc T2S machinery, shown to associate with secretin XpsD (Lee et al 2004 cross-linking, PMID 15590656).

machinery_genes.quote: The last ORF of an xps gene cluster, designated xpsD, is required for the secretion of extracellular enzymes across the outer membrane in Xanthomonas campestris pv. campestris.
machinery_genes.note: Hu et al 1995 (Microbiology); XpsD = outer-membrane lipoprotein secretin (GspD family), shown to form a multimer (Chen et al 1996, PMID 8576244).

machinery_genes.role: type IV prepilin/pseudopilin peptidase (matures pseudopilins; shared with type IV pilus)
machinery_genes.quote: Type IV pre-pilin leader peptidase was demonstrated to be required for protein secretion ... Sequencing of the cloned fragment revealed an open reading frame (ORF) (designated xpsO)
machinery_genes.note: Hu et al 1995 (Mol Microbiol); XpsO = the Xcc type IV prepilin peptidase (PilD/XcpA/GspO homolog), required for T2S and processes pseudopilins. Encoded outside the xpsEFGHIJKLMND cluster but tied to the Xps apparatus (analogous to VcpD/PilD in the V. cholerae pilot).

notes: Xps = the canonical Xanthomonas campestris pv. campestris type II secretion system, characterized in depth by the Hu/Leu/Chen group (National Chung Hsing University) in strain XC1701/XC17433. The apparatus paper strain is the same X. campestris pv. campestris species/pathovar as instance strain 8004; the xps locus is conserved across Xcc strains, so component naming transfers directly. The core secretion gene cluster is an 11-ORF operon named xpsEFGHIJKLMND (Lee et al 2001, PMID 11133946): cytoplasmic ATPase XpsE, IM platform XpsF/XpsL/XpsM/XpsN(GspC), major pseudopilin XpsG, minor pseudopilins XpsH/XpsI/XpsJ/XpsK, and the outer-membrane secretin XpsD. XpsO (type IV prepilin peptidase, PilD/GspO homolog) is encoded outside this cluster but is required for Xps secretion and matures the pseudopilins, so it is included as machinery (same treatment as VcpD/PilD in the V. cholerae pilot T2SS_01). XpsK is the only gene without an individual functional-characterization quote; it is documented only as a named ORF of the xpsEFGHIJKLMND cluster and as a member of the GspG/H/I/J/K pseudopilin family, flagged as lowest-confidence. Deliberately excluded: the secreted substrates/effectors (alpha-amylase, protease, pectate lyase, cellulase, e.g. effector XC_0532), the upstream Sec/Tat inner-membrane translocon (general IM transport, not Xps apparatus), and any transcriptional regulators. No separate XcsC-type or XpsA/XpsB accessory genes are described for this cluster (it runs E-N + D, plus the trans-encoded peptidase XpsO). All quotes verbatim from PubMed abstracts / PMC full text of the cited papers; PMIDs and DOIs confirmed via get_article_metadata.
